He added that the remaining…" ⚡️ 🇯🇵 🇮🇷 Kyodo: A Japanese journalist detained in Iran since January has been released on bail but remains unable to leave the country. The individual, identified as the Tehran bureau chief of public broadcaster NHK, is facing security-related charges and is expected to stand trial. Japan’s Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara confirmed the release, stating the journalist was freed on Monday and later met by Japan’s ambassador, who reported no health concerns. Tokyo has urged Iran to allow the individual to return home. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷Axios: Strikes before the end of the deadline cannot be ruled out. "First, I don't rule out at all that strikes could happen before the deadline. "So I think the Israeli prime minister, the Israeli government is very invested in this war. It's very invested in regime change in Iran. And I from what I hear from Israeli officials, prime minister Netanyahu expressed concern to president Trump on Sunday about the possibility of a ceasefire now. Netanyahu told Trump that there are risks in going to a ceasefire now, especially that according to Netanyahu, Iran might agree to do some semi concessions now, but a ceasefire if if if you go into a ceasefire Mhmm. It could lead to a permanent end to the war. I think that was Netanyahu's main argument that once The US stops, it will be very hard to resume the fighting. Therefore, I think he urged Trump to continue." @wfwitness
